title: Ecommerce Website
description: A mock ecommerce platform I built to get hands-on experience with full-stack development. It's got all the basics you'd expect from an online store — products, a shopping cart, and order handling. I focused on making the backend do the heavy lifting, so product management and the cart system are all driven from there. I also threw in some OAuth authentication to learn how real login systems work. Nothing too fancy, but it really helped me understand how frontend and backend talk to each other in a real application.

title: Vault
description: A personal budgeting app built to take control of your finances. You can set up and manage your budget, track expenses, and get a clear picture of where your money is going. The goal was to make budgeting feel less like a chore by keeping the interface clean and the data easy to read at a glance.
image1description: Budget setup page where you configure your monthly budget
image2description: Expenses page for tracking and reviewing spending

title: Task App
description: A simple task manager I built as part of my Personal Development Plan to dive into mobile development. I went with React Native so I could learn how mobile apps connect to a backend. It does what you'd expect — you can add tasks, mark them done, and everything saves between sessions. Nothing groundbreaking, but it was a great way to learn the ins and outs of building something that runs on a phone while still having a proper backend.

title: Smart Time Registration
description: A time tracking tool that takes the hassle out of logging hours. Instead of manually entering everything, you can import your Outlook calendar and it'll automatically link meetings and events to projects. We hooked it up with Microsoft authentication so organizations can use it securely. The idea came from seeing how much time people waste just tracking their time — this makes it way easier. Built this one with a team at university.
image1description: Home page showing imported calendar data

title: StreamFlix
description: A Netflix-inspired streaming platform I built to really dig into backend development. It handles user accounts, lets you build a watchlist, and serves up content through a proper API. I wanted to understand how streaming services work behind the scenes — things like authentication, managing user data, and keeping track of what people want to watch. The backend was the main focus here.

title: Bin There, Done That
description: An educational web app that teaches kids about recycling through mini-games. We wanted to make learning about waste separation actually fun, so we built interactive games where players sort trash into the right bins. There's even an AI feature that helps identify what type of waste something is. It's simple, colorful, and hopefully gets the message across in a way that sticks. This was a group project focused on making sustainability education more engaging.

title: SSM Product Passport Tracking
description: A product tracking system we built for a university group project. The idea was to create a 'passport' for products — kind of like a digital record that follows an item through the supply chain. You can see where a product came from, check its sustainability credentials, and track where it's been. We used UNS-inspired data principles to keep everything organized and traceable. It's the sort of thing that could help with supply chain transparency, letting manufacturers, suppliers, and customers all verify product details from one place.

title: NHL Stenden Explorer
description: A virtual campus tour app for NHL Stenden's Emmen campus. We built this so students, staff, parents, and visitors could explore the campus without actually being there. The coolest part is the 360-degree photo viewer — we went around campus with a 360 camera and integrated all the photos so people can look around like they're actually standing there. There's also an FAQ section with all the info people usually need about the campus facilities. Built this with a team, and it's designed to make campus info accessible to anyone, anytime.
image1description: 360-degree photo viewer for virtual campus exploration
